data.narrative:
Spartan is a Series B-stage defense & security company that has raised $42M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set is anchored by five named comparables: Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total capital raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $80M. Peer-median analysis references the median valuation across six same-sector, same-stage private peers at $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The median valuation across both methods implies a midpoint of $640M, with a low bound of $68M and a high bound of $1.4B.

The wide range reflects a material gap between the stage-progression signal ($80M) and peer comparables ($1.2B median). This divergence likely reflects either material qualitative differentiation relative to peers or inherent uncertainty in applying early-stage capital deployment as a valuation anchor. Resolution requires assessment of Spartan's revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical moat relative to the named peer set.
